routers.purpose: Account balances, aggregate value, portfolio operations; target_currency validation uses SUPPORTED_TARGET_CURRENCIES from portfolio_conversion_service (BTC, USD, USDC, USDT, ETH)
Historical account value snapshots for charting, daily activity markers (trades, deposits, withdrawals)

Read-only endpoints for trading accounts: listings, individual retrieval, portfolio/balance/bot queries, settings reads (auto-buy, rebalance, dust sweep, perps). v2.164.6 adds GET /{account_id}/account-value-summary (fast totals for the dashboard/header, backed by account_value_summary_service) and an include_details query flag on GET /{account_id}/portfolio that returns totals only when false. v2.165.0 adds GET /{account_id}/speculative-bucket returning the speculative bucket snapshot (bucket_usd, used_usd, remaining_usd, utilization_pct, open_speculative_positions, speculative_allocation_pct) via speculative_bucket_service.get_speculative_bucket_info for the dashboard bucket card. Request/response schemas come from app.schemas.accounts; TTL caches for live Coinbase API calls come from app.services.account_cache (both extracted from this file in v2.164.2 to break the cross-router import).

Write endpoints for trading accounts: creation, update, deletion (MFA-gated), default account selection, settings updates (auto-buy, rebalance, dust sweep), perps portfolio linking, dust sweep execution. Imports Pydantic schemas from app.schemas.accounts and the shared TTL cache dict from app.services.account_cache; no longer reaches into accounts_query_router. v2.165.0 adds POST /{account_id}/speculative-calibration/dismiss which validates a JWT dismiss-token (speculative_calibration_token.decode_dismiss_token) from the calibration alert email and writes speculative_calibration_last_alerted_at to stamp the 30-day cooldown; AccountUpdate also accepts speculative_allocation_pct (0-50% cap enforced in the schema).
RBAC administration: user management, group CRUD with role assignments, role CRUD with permission assignments, permission listing, session policy management, user session management
Manage per-user AI provider API keys

GET /api/ai/cost-summary?days=7 returns per-(provider, model) and per-provider aggregates of AI usage and cost for the current user over the requested window; scoped to current_user.id; rejects days < 1 or > 365
Thin aggregator that includes all auth sub-routers (auth_routers package)
Coin blacklist and category management
Real-time chat channels (DM, group, channel), message CRUD with soft delete, read tracking, member management, pinned messages, emoji reactions, message search
Aggregator for bot sub-routers (CRUD, control, logs, validation)
Proxy and cache cryptocurrency icons to avoid CORS

Current prices, OHLCV candles (cached), product listings; GET /api/products account ownership check uses accessible_accounts_filter (owner OR active member) and runs outside the broad try/except so HTTPException(404) is not swallowed as 500
Aggregated crypto news, video feeds, article content, source management

Market sentiment & blockchain metrics endpoints (Fear & Greed, BTC dominance, etc.); all 10+ handlers use try/except with stale cache fallback so a live-fetch failure returns the last cached value rather than a 500

Text-to-speech generation, audio caching, voice subscriptions; voices include Microsoft Edge TTS Neural voices and 7 added Neural/Multilingual Neural variants: davis, nancy, tony, ava-multi, emma-multi, brian-multi, andrew-multi
Successful and failed order history with filtering
Virtual paper trading account management
Aggregator for position sub-routers (queries, actions, limit orders, manual ops, perps, panic sell)
Market seasonality toggle for automatic bot management
User settings, changelog, system configuration
News and video source subscription management
Available trading strategies and parameter definitions
Health check, dashboard stats, monitor control, system status
Bot template CRUD
Manual buy/sell orders for portfolio management
PropGuard safety monitoring: status, kill switch, reset, history

Report generation endpoints, expense item CRUD (supports both expense and savings_target item types), expense categories, goal trend data with auto-backfill; ExpenseItemCreate/ExpenseItemUpdate schemas carry savings fields; list_expense_items returns dict (not List[dict]); split from reports_router.py. MUST be included before reports_crud_router in main.py so specific string-typed routes like /api/reports/expense-categories match before the int-typed /{report_id} catch-all (see TestReportsRouteOrdering in tests/routers/test_reports_router.py).

Financial goals CRUD, report schedules CRUD, generated report listing and download; _goal_to_dict() returns savings_target_count; split from reports_router.py. Registered AFTER reports_generation_router in main.py because its /{report_id} int-typed route would otherwise shadow sibling string routes.
Deposit/withdrawal tracking: sync transfers from Coinbase, list with filtering, manual entry, summary stats
Set/update display name, uniqueness check
Donation self-reporting and admin confirmation with monthly goal progress
Friend requests, friendships, blocking, user search
Game history, results, privacy settings
Multiplayer tournament CRUD and management
Active session listing and termination (S08 IDOR fix applied)

Account sharing / co-management: invite members (manager or shadow role), list members, update member role, remove member, leave account, resend invite, cancel invite; user-scoped inbound invitation endpoints (list/accept/decline). Invitations are gated by InviteRequest(email, role) where role is 'manager' or 'shadow'. Hardened in v2.164.0: rate-limited via user_rate_limit (invite: 10/hr per account + 30/hr per inviter; token actions: 30/hr per user) and invitation-token error responses collapsed to a single generic 'Invalid or expired invitation.' to prevent token-state enumeration. Delegates to services.account_sharing_service for business logic.

bot_routers.purpose: Bot creation, reading, updating, deletion, strategy listing. v2.165.0 adds _apply_risk_preset_defaults() which is called on create/update to merge preset defaults (from indicators.risk_presets) into the bot's strategy_config whenever a risk_preset is selected — notably auto-populating the speculative_mode, target_multiple, target_horizon_hours, and speculative_max_hold_hours fields for the 'speculative' preset.
Bot activation, deactivation, and force-run operations

Indicator condition evaluation log retrieval. v2.164.4: access uses accessible_account_ids (owner OR active member) so shared members can see bot reasoning; falls back to Bot.user_id == current_user.id when the user has no accessible accounts.
Scanner/monitor log retrieval for non-AI strategies
Bot config validation against exchange minimums

Bot budget rebalancer: GET /api/bots/rebalancer returns currency groups with participating bots for an account (v2.164.4: bot list is now per-account, not per-user, so shared members see the full rebalancer view); PUT /api/bots/rebalancer saves group settings and writes budget_percentage to all participating bots; PUT remains owner-only (Account.user_id == current_user.id).
Pydantic request/response schemas for bot routes

position_routers.purpose: Position listing (with offset/limit pagination, default limit 500), details, trades, AI logs, P&L timeseries; populates coin_category on each position from BlacklistedCoin reason prefix (e.g. [APPROVED], [MEME], [BLACKLISTED]); GET /api/positions/{position_id}/ai-opinion returns the most recent AIOpinionLog row (including tool_calls[]) for the position, scoped to accessible_account_ids. v2.164.5: returns 200 with null body when no opinion is logged yet instead of 404 (the Positions page prefetches per row; 404s produced browser-console spam for the common empty case). 404 is now reserved for actually-inaccessible or nonexistent positions. v2.164.10: the list endpoint no longer hydrates full trades/pending-order collections for every row; it now uses aggregate queries for trade counts, buy-price bounds, pending counts, targeted limit-close order lookups, page-scoped blacklist fetches, and narrower bot preloads for resize-budget calculation. v2.164.11: the hot list route now also reuses aggregated first-buy quote amounts for resize-budget hints and omits fill-by-fill limit-close history from the list payload, keeping only the summary fields the Positions cards render. v2.164.12: the list route also trims strategy_config_snapshot down to the subset of frozen config keys the open-positions UI actually reads, while the single-position detail route continues to return the full snapshot. v2.164.13: the open-position list now also clears several optional fields that the active-deals UI never reads (account_id, user_attempt_number, close/profit-at-close values, limit_close_order_id), while detail and closed-position paths keep them.
Cancel, force-close, and update position settings
Limit order operations (create, cancel, slippage check)
Manual operations: add funds, update notes

Emergency liquidation: POST /api/positions/panic-sell initiates background task with 4 ordered phases: (1) stopping_bots — stop active bots, (2) stopping_rebalancers — disable rebalancers/auto-buy/zero reserves (must precede position closing so rebalancers cannot buy into other bases during sells), (3) closing_positions — cancel or market-sell open positions (resets closing_via_limit before selling; passes force_market=True to bypass profit check), (4) converting — optional portfolio conversion to target currency; GET /api/positions/panic-sell-status/{task_id} polls in-memory _panic_tasks dict for phase progress; POST /api/positions/panic-sell-send-mfa sends email OTP or returns TOTP method; MFA verification required (TOTP or email OTP with token_type='action_mfa'); PanicSellRequest fields: account_id, action, target_currency, stop_bots, stop_portfolio_rebalancer, stop_bot_rebalancer, stop_auto_buy, zero_min_balances, mfa_code, confirm
Shared dependency functions for position routes

Shared helper functions (budget resize computation) used by multiple position sub-routers. v2.164.11: compute_resize_budget() can accept an explicit base-order size so hot list callers can avoid touching trade collections when they already have aggregated first-buy data.
Pydantic request/response schemas for position routes

news_data_modules.purpose: News and video source configurations by category

Cache loading/saving for news and video data; all loader functions (_load_market_metrics_cache and all 8 metric wrappers) accept a stale=True parameter to return the last persisted value without triggering a live fetch
Pydantic models for news router API

auth_routers.purpose: Pydantic request/response schemas for all auth endpoints
Shared helpers: password hashing, JWT creation, device trust, IP geolocation via ip-api.com (city/region/country per login event), MFA login completion
In-memory rate limiting for login, signup, forgot-password, resend verification, MFA verification
Core auth endpoints: login, register, logout, refresh, /me, change-password; rate limiting applied to /refresh and /change-password endpoints
TOTP MFA: setup, verify-setup, disable, verify during login
Email MFA: verify-email-code, verify-email-link, enable, disable, resend; disable endpoint requires TOTP gate; resend endpoint has rate limiting
Trusted device management: list, revoke, revoke-all

models.name: RevokedToken
models.purpose: Revoked JWT tokens for server-side token invalidation on logout and password change

models.name: Group
models.purpose: Organizational group for RBAC; users belong to groups, groups are assigned roles; session_policy (JSON) defines session limits for the group

models.name: Role
models.purpose: Functional capability set with associated permissions for RBAC

models.name: Permission
models.purpose: Granular permission using resource:action naming (e.g. bots:read, admin:users). All valid permission strings are enumerated in the Perm StrEnum (app/auth/dependencies.py) — use Perm constants in route declarations to prevent typos. Permissions cover: bots, positions, orders, accounts, reports, templates, settings, blacklist, news, system (monitor/restart/shutdown), admin (users/groups/roles/permissions), games (play/multiplayer), social (chat).

models.name: ActiveSession
models.purpose: Tracks active user sessions for enforcement of session limits (max simultaneous, per-IP, timeout)

models.name: Account
models.purpose: Trading account (CEX/DEX/paper/prop). v2.165.0 adds speculative_allocation_pct (0-50% cap, default 0; caps the 'speculative' bucket used by the high-risk doubling preset) and speculative_calibration_last_alerted_at (timestamp stamped when a calibration alert is acknowledged — drives the 30-day per-user cooldown in speculative_calibration_monitor).

models.name: AIOpinionLog
models.purpose: Per-call audit log of AI indicator (ai_spot_opinion) decisions. Written after every AISpotOpinionEvaluator.evaluate() call; outcome / realized_pnl_pct / closed_at are backfilled by the POSITION_CLOSED event-bus subscriber once the trade resolves. Read by the get_prior_ai_signals tool so the model can see how its own recent calls on a pair played out. model_used / input_tokens / output_tokens / cost_usd (added in migration 081) record the resolved model ID and per-call token usage + estimated USD cost, powering the Settings AI cost dashboard. doubling_probability_score / speculative_score / speculative_components (added in migration 083) store the AI-reported 0-100 likelihood of target_multiple hitting within target_horizon_hours plus the deterministic catalyst scorer output (total score + per-component breakdown from indicators.speculative_signals) for calibration analysis and the AI reasoning UI. 90-day retention via cleanup_old_ai_opinion_logs.

models.name: BotProduct
models.purpose: Junction table normalizing bot trading pairs (1NF)

models.name: BotTemplateProduct
models.purpose: Junction table normalizing template trading pairs (1NF)

models.name: ArticleTTS
models.purpose: Cached TTS audio per article and voice, shared across users

models.name: UserVoiceSubscription
models.purpose: Per-user TTS voice preferences

models.name: UserArticleTTSHistory
models.purpose: Per-user last-played voice per article for auto-resume

models.name: UserContentSeenStatus
models.purpose: Tracks which articles/videos each user has seen

models.name: ReportGoal
models.purpose: User financial targets (balance, profit, income, expenses) with time horizons for report tracking; expenses goals use expense_period and tax_withholding_pct

models.name: ExpenseItem
models.purpose: Itemized living expenses or savings targets for expense-type goals. item_type is 'expense' (default) or 'savings_target'. Savings targets carry a goal amount, target date, optional growth rate, current balance, and optional recurring recurrence; the PMT formula in expense_service computes the required monthly contribution. Expenses are normalized to the goal period for coverage waterfall calculations.

models.name: ReportSchedule
models.purpose: Report delivery configuration — when and how reports are generated

models.name: ReportScheduleGoal
models.purpose: Junction table linking report schedules to goals (many-to-many)

models.name: Report
models.purpose: Generated report instances with metrics, HTML, PDF, and AI summary

models.name: AccountTransfer
models.purpose: Deposit/withdrawal tracking for accurate P&L calculation; synced from Coinbase API or entered manually

models.name: GoalProgressSnapshot
models.purpose: Daily goal progress data points for trend line visualization, captured during account snapshot cycle

models.name: Friendship
models.purpose: Bidirectional friend relationship; stored as two rows per friendship (A->B and B->A)

models.name: FriendRequest
models.purpose: Pending friend request; deleted on accept or reject

models.name: BlockedUser
models.purpose: User block relationship; prevents friend requests from blocked user

models.name: GameResult
models.purpose: Persistent record of a completed multiplayer game

models.name: GameResultPlayer
models.purpose: Per-player result within a game

models.name: GameHistoryVisibility
models.purpose: Per-user privacy control for game history sharing (all_friends, opponents_only, private)

models.name: GameHighScore
models.purpose: Per-user best score for each game

models.name: Tournament
models.purpose: Multi-game tournament among friends; status: pending, active, completed

models.name: TournamentPlayer
models.purpose: Player enrolled in a tournament with score tracking

models.name: TournamentDeleteVote
models.purpose: Committee vote for tournament deletion; all players must vote to delete

models.name: ChatChannel
models.purpose: Chat channel: DM (2 users), group (N users), or channel (open/admin-created)

models.name: ChatChannelMember
models.purpose: Membership in a chat channel with role (owner/admin/member) and read tracking

models.name: ChatMessage
models.purpose: Message in a chat channel; supports edit, soft delete, reply threading, and pin

models.name: ChatMessageReaction
models.purpose: Emoji reaction on a chat message; unique per user+message+emoji

models.name: RateLimitAttempt
models.purpose: Persists rate limit tracking across restarts

models.name: Donation
models.purpose: Self-report -> admin confirm workflow for donations

models_note: Models are split into domain files under backend/app/models/: auth.py, trading.py, reporting.py, social.py, content.py, donations.py, system.py. All tables live in named PostgreSQL schemas matching their domain (auth, trading, reporting, social, content, system) as of migration 068_domain_schemas.py. Foreign keys across schemas use fully-qualified strings (e.g. 'auth.users.id').

services.purpose: Shared filter utilities for multi-user account access — SQLAlchemy filter clauses and helper functions that check both ownership (account.user_id) and active membership (AccountMembership with non-expired expires_at). Supports 'manager' (read+write) and 'shadow' (read-only) roles. Used across routers and services to enforce tenant isolation + shared-access semantics consistently.

In-memory TTL caches keyed by account_id for live-exchange endpoints (_TTL_REBALANCE_STATUS 30s, _TTL_DUST_SWEEP 60s). Shared by both accounts routers — the query router populates and serves; the mutation router invalidates on settings change. Protects the account owner's Coinbase API rate quota when multiple members of a shared account hit the same live endpoint. Extracted from accounts_query_router in v2.164.2 to break the cross-router import from accounts_mutation_router.

Shared response schemas and builders for account endpoints: RebalanceSettingsResponse pydantic DTO and build_rebalance_response(account) factory. Used by both accounts_query_router and accounts_mutation_router; extracted from accounts_query_router in Phase 5.3 so neither router has to reach into the other's private helpers.

Account business logic: prop firm config validation, exchange account creation with credential encryption and connectivity testing, per-account portfolio retrieval. get_portfolio_for_account() accepts include_details=False to skip per-asset holdings expansion and CEX paper-portfolio price fanouts were moved to paper_valuation_service in v2.164.6 (module trimmed to the orchestration path only).

Business logic for account co-management: creating/validating invitations (7-day TTL), accepting/declining/revoking invitations (single-use tokens, email match enforcement), managing memberships (add, change role, remove), and audit event logging via AccountMembershipEvent. Enforces role whitelist ('manager'/'shadow' only, never 'owner') and prevents account owner removal via membership delete. v2.164.4: list_members takes caller_role and redacts peer emails (and inviter email fallback) unless the caller is the owner, so sharing the account doesn't leak members' emails to each other.
Captures daily account value snapshots for historical charting; triggers goal progress snapshot capture; aggregates daily activity (closed trades, transfers) for chart markers

Fast dashboard/header value totals per account (get_account_value_summary) — 60s api_cache with force_fresh bypass. Paper accounts prefer the most recent AccountValueSnapshot (returned with is_stale=true) while a bounded-concurrency live rebuild runs in the background via _schedule_paper_summary_refresh / _refresh_paper_summary_cache; CEX accounts call get_cex_portfolio(include_details=False); other backends fall back to get_portfolio_for_account. Introduced in v2.164.6 so first-paint doesn't block on the full portfolio payload.
Per-user AI provider credential management and retrieval
AI-powered grid parameter optimization

Article content extraction: fetches article body from external URLs with robots.txt compliance, caches in DB (article_content_cache column), per-user rate limiting (30/hr), trafilatura-based extraction; uses threading.Lock (not asyncio.Lock) for cache synchronization; router is a thin wrapper over this service
Auto-converts stablecoins to BTC when balances exceed minimums

Banned IP monitoring via fail2ban with geo-lookup enrichment using ip-api.com batch endpoint (free, 100 IPs/request, full country names returned directly); daily snapshot via APScheduler; geo results cached per-IP to avoid re-lookups across refreshes
Calculates PnL, win rate, trades/day, budget utilization, and daily PnL projections for bot listings
Validates quote currency consistency, auto-corrects AI bot market focus, and validates bidirectional budget config
White-label branding configuration and customization
BroadcastBackend protocol + InProcessBroadcast + RedisBroadcast; Phase 3 multi-process WebSocket fan-out seam (v2.128.0)
Calculates available capital for bidirectional bots with reservations

Chat business logic: channel creation/management, message lifecycle (send, edit, soft delete), read tracking, membership with roles (owner/admin/member), emoji reactions, pinned messages, message search

Real-time WebSocket chat routing: send, typing indicator (debounced), read receipt, message edit/delete, emoji reactions, pin toggle; accepts session_maker injection; uses registry.broadcast for fan-out
Weekly AI-powered coin status review and categorization
Periodically fetches news and video content from configured sources
CredentialsProvider protocol seam for Phase 3 credentials microservice (v2.130.0)
Weekly check for US debt ceiling legislation changes

Daily sync of trading pairs; removes delisted pairs from bots (checks both product_ids list and legacy product_id field); detects unresolvable paper-account coin balances via _get_unresolvable_coins() and publishes the result to the module-level _unresolvable_paper_coins set so paper_trading_client can suppress repeated 404 pricing requests
Fetches wallet balances from blockchain networks (Ethereum, L2s)
Detects throwaway email addresses at registration
Domain-level blocking for content sources that block our IP

AWS SES email sending for verification, password reset, MFA codes. v2.165.0 adds send_speculative_calibration_email() with HTML/plain body builders that include verbatim copy-paste markers for the calibration alert so users can review the recommendation in email and click the signed dismiss link back into Settings.

Speculative bucket math and calibration analysis for the high-risk doubling preset. get_speculative_bucket_info(account) returns bucket_usd (account_value * speculative_allocation_pct), used_usd (sum of open speculative-bucket position cost basis), remaining_usd, utilization_pct, and open_speculative_positions. validate_speculative_entry(account, proposed_notional_usd) is the preflight bucket check wired into trading_engine.signal_processor.buy_decision so a new speculative position cannot exceed the configured cap. analyze_speculative_calibration(account) aggregates closed speculative trades to produce the win-rate vs target-multiple-hit stats and recommended allocation used by the daily calibration monitor.

Daily periodic task (24h interval) that runs analyze_speculative_calibration for every account with speculative_allocation_pct > 0, compares actual vs target hit-rate, and emails a calibration alert (via email_service.send_speculative_calibration_email) when the allocation should be adjusted. Enforces a 30-day per-user cooldown via Account.speculative_calibration_last_alerted_at so users aren't spammed. Also broadcasts a 'speculative_calibration_alert' WebSocket event to the account owner so the frontend can show a persistent toast with a 'Review in email' CTA. Wired into main.py startup/shutdown via start_speculative_calibration_monitor / stop_speculative_calibration_monitor.

JWT dismiss-token helpers for the speculative calibration alert email. create_dismiss_token(account_id, user_id) issues a short-lived signed token, decode_dismiss_token(token) validates it for the POST /api/accounts/{id}/speculative-calibration/dismiss endpoint, and build_dismiss_url(account_id, user_id) produces the Settings deep link embedded in the email body (frontend reads ?dismiss_token=&account_id= on mount and posts back to dismiss).
Per-user, per-account exchange client instantiation with async locking and double-checked caching

Expense item normalization (frequency to period), PMT-formula monthly savings contribution calculation (compute_monthly_savings_contribution()), savings target entry builder (_build_savings_target_entry()), and two-pass coverage waterfall for expense-type goals. Recurring savings targets use a self-sustaining hold model: _compute_self_sustaining_hold() computes the minimum seed to keep after each withdrawal so it compounds back to cover the next cycle without further contributions. _compute_gross_target() uses max(current_balance, self_sustaining_hold) as the hold component for recurring items.
Real-time WebSocket notifications for friend events
In-memory game room state for multiplayer sessions
WebSocket handler for multiplayer game messages
Captures daily goal progress snapshots (balance, profit, expense types), backfills from AccountValueSnapshot history and closed-position income, provides trend data for charting
Periodic profit lock-in and grid refresh for winning positions
Grid bot lifecycle: initialization, level management, order placement; GridRebalanceParams dataclass used for parameterizing rebalance operations
Logs indicator condition evaluations for non-AI bots
Monitors pending limit orders and updates positions on fill

External API fetch functions for market sentiment and blockchain metrics (Fear & Greed, BTC dominance, altseason, mempool, hash rate, lightning, ATH, RSI, stablecoin mcap, US debt); includes metric snapshot recording and pruning
Fetches individual article content from source URLs with robots.txt compliance
Business logic for news article queries and response assembly: database query functions, article-to-response-dict transformation, paginated response assembly; extracted from news_router.py
Downloads and saves news thumbnails as WebP files to backend/news_images/
Detects and fixes positions with missing fill data; includes MissingOrderDetector

Shared paper-account valuation helpers used by both the dashboard summary and the Portfolio page so totals stay consistent without hammering Coinbase. build_paper_holdings_and_totals(account) loads JSON paper_balances, fetches the public BTC/USD price, then prices each non-stable asset with an asyncio.Semaphore(PAPER_PRICE_CONCURRENCY=5) — USD pair first, BTC pair fallback — and returns holdings + total_usd_value/total_btc_value. Stables (USD/USDC/USDT) short-circuit to $1. Extracted from account_service._build_paper_portfolio in v2.164.6.
Syncs perpetual futures positions with Coinbase INTX state; accepts session_maker injection for testability

Pure, side-effect-free portfolio computation helpers extracted from portfolio_service (Phase 5): _build_portfolio_holdings (handles both owner-scoped and aggregated-cross-account paths via optional open_positions), _compute_position_pnl, _apply_asset_pnl_to_holdings, _compute_balance_breakdown (with BalanceBreakdownParams dataclass), _compute_closed_pnl. Four shadow helpers (_process_cex_holdings, _apply_unrealized_pnl, _calculate_balance_breakdown, _calculate_realized_pnl) were removed in v2.164.3; both call paths in portfolio_service now use the single canonical set. No DB, cache, or API calls.

Portfolio conversion with progress tracking; SUPPORTED_TARGET_CURRENCIES = {BTC, USD, USDC, USDT, ETH}; _FALLBACK_INTERMEDIATE dict defines intermediate routing for each target; _sell_currency_with_fallback and _convert_intermediate_currency extended to handle all 5 target currencies; init_task() added for deferred task initialization

Portfolio aggregation and balance calculations; public async fetchers and the global cache/API symbols. Pure computation helpers moved to portfolio_calculations.py in Phase 5 (module shrunk from 1241 LOC to 786 LOC). v2.164.6: get_cex_portfolio accepts include_details=False to return totals only (skips the open-position query, per-asset PnL/balance fan-out, and holdings list) so the fast summary path can reuse it without paying for full portfolio assembly.
P&L calculation for long/short positions: calculate_profit() returns profit_quote, profit_pct, unrealized_value; extracted from Position model
Background equity monitoring for prop firm accounts every 30s
Portfolio rebalance trigger monitoring; 30s APScheduler interval

Pure business logic for computing portfolio allocation percentages, reserve values, and deployable pools; uses bulk list_products() pricing (cached 1hr) instead of per-asset ticker calls; extracted from accounts_router.py

Shared access-control helpers for report goals, schedules, and reports: get_accessible_goal (owner or observer/manager), get_writable_goal (owner or manager), get_writable_schedule, report_to_dict. Used by both reports_crud_router and reports_generation_router; extracted from reports_crud_router in Phase 5.3.
Generates AI-powered summaries for performance reports using per-user AI credentials; tiered output (beginner/comfortable/experienced); includes trading activity context in prompts

Gathers metrics for report generation: account value snapshots, closed positions, profit calculations, goal progress, trade summary stats, prior period comparisons; financial helper functions extracted into this service

Report generation package. Modules (after Phase 5 split): html_builder.py (HTML report assembly + AI summaries), html_charts.py (SVG trend chart + minimap + value formatting, extracted from html_builder in Phase 5), chart_renderer.py + chart_pdf_renderer.py (chart rendering entry points), expense_schedule.py (date math + lookahead/upcoming helpers, extracted in Phase 5), expense_sections.py (HTML sections: coverage, savings, changes; savings target spend-line breakdown showing Spend · Tax · Hold → accumulate, Ready badge when fully funded), expense_card.py (goal-card header + tab assembly + render entry, extracted in Phase 5), pdf_generator.py (PDF generation with fpdf2, savings targets section via _build_pdf_savings_targets()). Package __init__.py re-exports the public surface. The former expense_builder.py was split into the three expense_* modules in Phase 5.
Report schedule CRUD business logic: create and update report schedules with goal associations; extracted from reports_router.py
Background task that checks for due report schedules and generates reports; also provides manual trigger function; DeliverReportParams dataclass used for report delivery parameterization
Market season detection using halving cycles and multiple indicators

Seasonality-based bot management: auto_manage_bots() enables/disables bots based on Risk-Off/Risk-On cycle mode (grid bots exempt), build_seasonality_response() assembles response dict; extracted from seasonality_router.py
Resolves effective session policy by merging group policies (most-restrictive) and applying per-user overrides
Session lifecycle management: create, validate, end, enforce limits (max simultaneous, per-IP), expire stale sessions
User settings retrieval and persistence
Tracks in-flight operations for graceful shutdown
Syncs deposit/withdrawal transactions from Coinbase into account_transfers table with deduplication by external_id

In-memory per-user sliding-window rate limiter (keyed by user_id + bucket). check_user_rate_limit enforces allow-counted caps for authenticated endpoints (e.g., invitations: 10/hr per account + 30/hr per inviter, invitation-token actions: 30/hr per user). record_user_failure / clear_user_failures gates brute-force-sensitive operations (MFA verify: 5 failures / 15 min). Complements the IP-based PublicEndpointRateLimiter. Single-process; swap in Redis for multi-process deployments.
WebSocket connection manager for real-time order fill notifications

trading_engine.coordinators_note: Top-level coordinators (trading_engine_v2.py, trading_client.py, multi_bot_monitor.py, order_validation.py, precision.py, product_precision.py) are listed in core_modules

modules.purpose: Signal processing and trading decision orchestration (Phase 5 package, split from a 1308-LOC monolith). __init__.py exports process_signal() orchestrator; _shared.py holds context/budget/indicator/signal-log helpers; buy_decision.py holds _decide_buy + _execute_buy_trade (Phase 5.2 helpers: _log_budget_blocker_if_applicable, _run_new_position_preflight, _evaluate_blacklist_category, new/DCA/stopped path handlers) and v2.165.0 adds a speculative-bucket preflight check via speculative_bucket_service.validate_speculative_entry so new speculative-preset entries cannot exceed the per-account speculative cap; sell_decision.py holds _decide_and_execute_sell (Phase 5.2 helpers: _build_market_context, _resolve_take_profit_mode, _verify_mark_profit_allows_sell, _execute_position_close).
Market and limit buy order execution; pipeline decomposed into discrete stages
Market and limit sell order execution; pipeline decomposed into discrete stages
Perpetual futures order execution with bracket TP/SL
Position CRUD operations and lifecycle management
Order logging, AI decision logging, audit trail
Trailing stop loss and trailing take profit management
Shared fill reconciliation logic with retry-based order fill retrieval and exponential backoff, used by buy_executor and sell_executor

monitor_modules.purpose: Batch analysis of multiple trading pairs using AI batch analysis (single API call); extracted from MultiBotMonitor.process_bot_batch()
Signal processing for a single bot/pair combination; extracted from MultiBotMonitor.process_bot_pair()
Bull flag strategy bot processing: volume spike scanning, pattern detection, trailing stop management; extracted from MultiBotMonitor.process_bull_flag_bot()

implementations.purpose: Wraps CoinbaseClient to implement ExchangeClient interface

Simulated exchange client for paper trading; _is_known_unresolvable() consults delisted_pair_monitor._unresolvable_paper_coins to skip get_price() calls for delisted coins, suppressing repeated 404 requests and downgrading those log lines to DEBUG
Wraps ByBitClient to implement ExchangeClient for ByBit V5 linear perps
Low-level pybit HTTP wrapper with product ID translation and async
ByBit WebSocket manager for real-time position/order/wallet/ticker streams
ExchangeClient for FTMO MT5 EA bridge via HTTP JSON
PropGuard safety decorator wrapping any ExchangeClient with drawdown checks
Pure helper functions for drawdown calculations and daily reset logic
DEX trading client for on-chain swaps
DEX contract addresses, router ABIs, chain configs

coinbase_modules.purpose: HMAC and CDP authentication for Coinbase API
Prices, candles, product listings with single-flight caching
Public (no-auth) market data endpoints for paper trading and fallback; includes PublicMarketDataClient

exchange_clients.unified_client: coinbase_unified_client.py (CoinbaseClient wrapper coordinating all API modules with CircuitBreaker)

strategies.purpose: Base strategy interface and registry for strategy discovery
DCA grid trading with limit orders at predetermined price levels

Unified condition-based strategy with configurable indicators for entry/DCA/exit. Pure helpers (flatten_conditions, needs_aggregate_indicators, build_ai_params, build_bull_flag_params) extracted to strategies/indicator_based_helpers.py in Phase 5 to stay under the 1200-LOC cap. v2.165.0 adds a time-based max-hold exit: when speculative_max_hold_hours is set in the strategy config, positions older than the threshold are force-exited regardless of take-profit/stop-loss (used by the 'speculative' risk preset for catalyst trades).

Pure, side-effect-free helpers extracted from IndicatorBasedStrategy in Phase 5: flatten_conditions(expression), needs_aggregate_indicators(base, safety, take_profit), build_ai_params(config), build_bull_flag_params(config). Callable without an instance.
Cross-exchange arbitrage exploiting price differences between venues
Mean-reversion strategy based on price correlations between pairs
Volume spike and bull flag pattern detection for USD pairs
Mirrors long conditions to short conditions for bidirectional bots

core_modules.purpose: FastAPI application entry point, startup/shutdown events, background task orchestration. v2.165.0 wires start_speculative_calibration_monitor / stop_speculative_calibration_monitor into the startup/shutdown hooks for the daily speculative-bucket calibration loop.
Unified AI client interface (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini)
In-memory cache with TTL, single-flight pattern (thundering herd prevention), and persistent portfolio cache

Scheduled database and in-memory cleanup: old decision logs, failed orders, noise logs, expired sessions, revoked tokens, rate limit attempts, AI opinion logs (90-day retention via cleanup_old_ai_opinion_logs), and periodic 5-minute in-memory cache sweep (price cache, chat/game rate-limit dicts, game rooms, monitor caches, WebSocket stale connections)
Flexible condition framework (indicator-based) with indicator comparisons
Pydantic settings loaded from .env (database URL, JWT secret, CORS, etc.)

Trading pairs, timeframes, cache TTLs, and other application constants. Includes PAIR_CONCURRENCY = 5 (semaphore limit for concurrent per-pair sessions in multi_bot_monitor), VALID_CATEGORIES (coin blacklist classification values: APPROVED, BORDERLINE, QUESTIONABLE, MEME, BLACKLISTED), and compute_dynamic_concurrency() for RAM-scaled bot/pair concurrency.
Multi-quote-currency detection and formatting (BTC/USD)
SQLAlchemy async engine, session maker, default content sources
Domain exceptions (AppError, ValidationError, NotFoundError, ExchangeUnavailableError) replacing HTTPException in services for framework decoupling
MultiFernet encryption for API keys and credentials at rest; supports comma-separated keys for key rotation (first key encrypts, all keys decrypt)
Calculates all technical indicators from candle data for conditions
Technical indicator calculators (MACD, RSI, Bollinger Bands, EMA, SMA)

SQLAlchemy ORM model definitions split into domain files: auth.py (users, groups, roles, permissions, sessions, tokens), trading.py (bots, positions, trades, signals, orders), reporting.py (goals, expenses, reports, snapshots, transfers), social.py (friends, chat, games, tournaments), content.py (news, videos, TTS, sources), donations.py, system.py. All tables live in named schemas matching their domain (auth, trading, reporting, social, content, system) as of migration 068_domain_schemas.py. Foreign keys across schemas use fully-qualified strings (e.g. 'auth.users.id').
Advanced condition grouping with AND/OR/NOT logic (phase-based)
Unified CoinbaseClient class coordinating all Coinbase API modules with rate limiting (150ms min between requests) and CircuitBreaker for API resilience (5-failure threshold, 60s recovery)

Seed data loaded by database.init_db() on startup (Phase 5 split from the 1337-LOC database_seeds.py). seeds/content_sources.py holds DEFAULT_CONTENT_SOURCES, DEAD_SOURCES, SOURCE_SCRAPE_POLICIES, and seed_default_sources(). seeds/coin_categories.py holds DEFAULT_COIN_CATEGORIES and seed_default_coins(). seeds/__init__.py re-exposes the public API. The old app/database_seeds.py file was removed.

Monitors prices for all active bots and dispatches strategy signals; concurrent per-pair processing via asyncio.gather() with asyncio.Semaphore(PAIR_CONCURRENCY=5) and per-pair DB sessions; sequential inter-pair batch sleep removed
Ensures orders meet exchange minimum size requirements before submission using product precision data
Shared filesystem path constants (BACKEND_DIR, TTS_CACHE_DIR) preventing cross-layer imports
Quote and base currency amount formatting with proper decimal precision for exchange API orders
Per-product increment specification lookup from Coinbase API precision data (JSON-backed, cached)
Quote-currency agnostic buy/sell wrapper that auto-detects quote currency and calls appropriate ExchangeClient methods

Strategy-based trading engine coordinator (StrategyTradingEngine) wrapping all trading engine modules; execute_sell() accepts force_market: bool = False and forwards it to sell_executor.execute_sell() to bypass the market-fallback profit check (used by panic sell)
APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler managing all non-realtime background jobs (replaced hand-rolled loops at v2.126.0)

In-process pub/sub event bus for domain events (order.filled, bot.started, etc.); NATS/Redis swap seam (v2.127.0). Topics include ORDER_FILLED, POSITION_OPENED, POSITION_CLOSED, BOT_STARTED, BOT_STOPPED, GOAL_ACHIEVED. POSITION_CLOSED producers (v2.159.0): services/limit_order_monitor.py, services/perps_monitor.py, monitor/bull_flag_processor.py, trading_engine/buy_executor.py, trading_engine/sell_executor.py, trading_engine/perps_executor.py. Subscriber ai_opinion_logger.on_position_closed backfills outcome/realized_pnl_pct on AIOpinionLog rows tied to the closed position.
ServiceRegistry injection point for four backend abstractions: event_bus, broadcast, rate_limiter, credentials (v2.131.0)

schemas.purpose: Shared Pydantic request/response schemas for the accounts routers: AccountBase, AccountCreate, AccountUpdate, AccountResponse, AccountWithBalance, AutoBuySettings, AutoBuySettingsUpdate, RebalanceSettingsUpdate, DustSweepSettingsUpdate. Extracted from accounts_query_router in v2.164.2 so accounts_mutation_router no longer cross-imports from another router. v2.165.0 adds speculative_allocation_pct to AccountUpdate (validated 0-50) and AccountResponse so the Settings page can read and persist the speculative bucket size.
Market data and signal Pydantic schemas
Position Pydantic response schemas; includes AIOpinionLogResponse (single ai_opinion_log row with tool_calls[]) served by GET /api/positions/{id}/ai-opinion; uses ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)

indicators_module.purpose: AISpotOpinionEvaluator: AI-powered indicator combining technical metrics with LLM reasoning. Pre-fetches static context (portfolio / position) into the prompt, then runs a multi-turn provider tool loop that exposes the argument-taking tools (get_candle_window, get_recent_news, get_trade_history, get_prior_ai_signals). AISpotOpinionParams.ai_model_override is plumbed through to get_provider(model=...) so bots can pin a specific model. _call_llm returns a 5-tuple (signal, confidence, reasoning, tool_calls, usage_meta) where usage_meta = {model_used, input_tokens, output_tokens, cost_usd} (cost computed via indicators.ai_pricing.estimate_cost_usd). Every evaluation is persisted via ai_opinion_logger.log_opinion with the usage/cost fields. v2.165.0 adds catalyst mode: params gain speculative_mode, target_multiple, and target_horizon_hours; when speculative_mode is on the evaluator runs the deterministic indicators.speculative_signals scorer as a prefilter and asks the model for a doubling_probability_score (0-100) alongside the signal, so only pairs that clear the catalyst threshold reach the LLM.

Writes one AIOpinionLog row per AISpotOpinionEvaluator.evaluate() call (fire-and-forget) and subscribes to the event bus POSITION_CLOSED topic to backfill outcome / realized_pnl_pct / closed_at when the trade resolves. log_opinion accepts and persists model_used / input_tokens / output_tokens / cost_usd so the Settings AI cost dashboard can aggregate spend per provider and model. v2.165.0 also persists doubling_probability_score, speculative_score, and speculative_components (from the catalyst prefilter) so the calibration monitor and the frontend AI reasoning expander can show the full signal breakdown. classify_outcome(pct) maps PnL% to win/loss/breakeven.

AI model pricing table (USD per 1M tokens, input/output) and cost helpers. MODEL_PRICING covers the known Claude, GPT, and Gemini family bases; estimate_cost_usd(model, input_tokens, output_tokens) uses longest-prefix matching so versioned model IDs fall back to their family's base row; provider_for_model(model) returns 'anthropic' / 'openai' / 'gemini' / 'unknown'. Unknown models cost 0.0 (logged but not blocking).
Bull flag chart pattern detection aggregate indicator

Default risk preset configurations for AI indicators. v2.165.0 adds the 'speculative' preset (high-risk doubling setup: enables speculative_mode + target_multiple, sets a short speculative_max_hold_hours catalyst window, wider stop-loss, and the bucket-aware budget sizing used by buy_decision's preflight).

Deterministic 0-100 catalyst scorer used as the prefilter for the 'speculative' risk preset. Aggregates 6 weighted components (momentum, volume, volatility expansion, news/social catalysts, relative strength, structural breakout) into a single speculative_score plus a speculative_components breakdown; the output is consumed by ai_spot_opinion (catalyst mode) and persisted on AIOpinionLog for calibration analysis.

ai_tools.description: Callable functions the AI spot-opinion indicator exposes to the LLM via tool-use. Each module registers a Tool instance into a shared REGISTRY at import time. Static (no-arg) tools are pre-fetched and injected into the prompt; argument-taking tools are offered to the model inside the provider tool loop.

base.purpose: Shared registry: Tool dataclass, ToolContext (db, user_id, product_id, current_price, bot, position, account_id, is_sell_check), REGISTRY, register(), get_schemas_for(), and execute() (fail-open — tool errors become {"error": ...} so the model loop never breaks).

tools.name: get_portfolio_context
tools.purpose: Other open positions on the same account: count by quote currency, total quote exposure, base-asset concentration, top holdings.

tools.name: get_position_context
tools.purpose: Currently-held position: entry price, minutes held, unrealized PnL %, DCA count, highest price since entry, configured stop-loss / take-profit targets.

tools.name: get_candle_window
tools.purpose: Fetch a compact OHLCV window on a requested Coinbase granularity so the model can zoom in on short-term volatility or out to a daily context.

tools.name: get_recent_news
tools.purpose: Return recent content.news_articles whose title or summary mentions the base asset of the current product (no external fetch).

tools.name: get_trade_history
tools.purpose: Recent closed positions for the current user on this product plus a compact summary (count, win rate, avg PnL %, avg hold minutes).

tools.name: get_prior_ai_signals
tools.purpose: Last N closed AI opinions for this user + product from trading.ai_opinion_log with outcome / realized_pnl_pct backfilled — lets the model see whether its own recent calls on this pair have paid off.

ai_providers.description: Adapters that implement the LLMProvider protocol (async call_with_tools(system, user, tools, tool_ctx, max_turns) -> (text, calls, usage)). The canonical tool schema is Anthropic's shape; each adapter translates it into its own call format, emits NormalizedToolCall records, and sums a TokenUsage(input_tokens, output_tokens) across every turn of the tool loop so upstream code (the evaluator, logger, cost dashboard) never branches on provider.

files.purpose: LLMProvider protocol, NormalizedToolCall dataclass, TokenUsage dataclass (input_tokens, output_tokens), get_provider(model=...) lookup, summarize_output() helper.
Claude adapter — native Anthropic tool-use format; accumulates usage.input_tokens / usage.output_tokens across tool-loop turns and returns TokenUsage.
OpenAI GPT adapter — translates canonical tool schemas to OpenAI function-call shape; accumulates prompt/completion token counts across turns and returns TokenUsage.
Google Gemini adapter — translates canonical tool schemas to Gemini function-declaration shape; accumulates usageMetadata prompt/candidates token counts across turns and returns TokenUsage.

price_feeds.purpose: DEX price feed implementation (Uniswap V3, etc.)
Combines multiple price feeds for best-price discovery

middleware.purpose: Adds 'Z' suffix to datetime fields in JSON responses
ASGI middleware that rate-limits public (unauthenticated) endpoints by client IP (120 req/60s); covers ticker, prices, candles, coin icons, version, and brand endpoints

Scans POST/PUT/PATCH bodies and GET query strings for injection patterns (SQL, XSS, shell, path traversal); logs attempts and triggers fail2ban after threshold — does NOT block requests to avoid false positives on legitimate content

security_headers: SecurityHeadersMiddleware (inline in main.py) sets X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. CSP and HSTS are intentionally NOT set here — they are owned by nginx (tradebot.conf) to avoid header stacking that would break TradingView chart embeds.

utils.purpose: Candle data utilities: timeframe aggregation, gap filling, synthetic timeframes
Checks robots.txt compliance before scraping article content
URL normalization, feed deduplication, and SSRF protection (validates URLs do not target internal/private/loopback/link-local addresses or AWS metadata endpoint)

auth_modules.purpose: FastAPI auth dependencies: get_current_user, require_account_access, permission/role gates; Perm StrEnum enumerating every valid resource:action permission string.

Shared verify_mfa(db, user, mfa_code) helper for sensitive actions (panic-sell, account deletion, etc.). Supports TOTP (authenticator app) and email MFA (EmailVerificationToken with token_type='action_mfa'); raises HTTPException(403) on failure; no-op when user has no MFA configured. Brute-force hardened (v2.164.0): 5 failed attempts within 15 minutes triggers an HTTPException(429) lockout via user_rate_limit.record_user_failure; success clears the failure counter. Extracted from panic_sell_router in Phase 5.3 so multiple routers can reuse it without cross-router imports.

ai_providers.name: Anthropic (Claude)

background_tasks_note: v2.126.0: Tiered scheduler architecture. Tier 1 (asyncio.create_task, real-time trading): MultiBotMonitor, LimitOrderMonitor, OrderReconciliationMonitor, MissingOrderDetector, PerpsMonitor, PropGuardMonitor. Tier 2 (APScheduler near-realtime): AutoBuyMonitor (10s), RebalanceMonitor (30s), TransferSync (24h), AccountSnapshotCapture (24h), BanMonitor (24h), ReportScheduler (15min). Tier 3 (APScheduler batch): ContentRefreshService, DomainBlacklistService, DebtCeilingMonitor, CoinReviewScheduler, TradingPairMonitor, cleanup jobs.

background_tasks.name: MultiBotMonitor
background_tasks.interval: Per-strategy (15min for AI, varies for others)
background_tasks.purpose: Core bot loop: parallel price monitoring and strategy signal dispatch (Semaphore(5))

background_tasks.name: LimitOrderMonitor
background_tasks.purpose: Check pending limit orders for fills

background_tasks.name: OrderReconciliationMonitor
background_tasks.purpose: Auto-fix orphaned positions with missing fill data

background_tasks.name: MissingOrderDetector
background_tasks.purpose: Detect unrecorded orders from exchange

background_tasks.name: PerpsMonitor
background_tasks.purpose: Sync perpetual futures positions with exchange state

background_tasks.name: PropGuardMonitor
background_tasks.purpose: Background equity monitoring for prop firm accounts

background_tasks.name: AutoBuyMonitor
background_tasks.purpose: Convert stablecoins to BTC when thresholds exceeded

background_tasks.name: RebalanceMonitor
background_tasks.purpose: Portfolio rebalance trigger monitoring

background_tasks.name: ReportScheduler
background_tasks.purpose: Check for due report schedules and generate reports

background_tasks.name: TransferSync
background_tasks.purpose: Sync deposit/withdrawal transactions from Coinbase

background_tasks.name: AccountSnapshotCapture
background_tasks.purpose: Capture account value snapshots for historical charts and goal progress snapshots

background_tasks.name: BanMonitor
background_tasks.purpose: Banned IP monitoring with geo-lookup enrichment (country, ISP, city) via ip-api.com

background_tasks.name: TradingPairMonitor
background_tasks.purpose: Sync trading pairs, remove delisted pairs from bots

background_tasks.name: ContentRefreshService
background_tasks.purpose: Fetch fresh news articles and YouTube videos

background_tasks.name: DebtCeilingMonitor
background_tasks.purpose: Check for new US debt ceiling legislation

background_tasks.name: CoinReviewScheduler
background_tasks.purpose: Weekly AI-powered coin status review and categorization

background_tasks.name: DomainBlacklistService
background_tasks.purpose: Domain-level blocking refresh for content sources that block our IP

background_tasks.name: DecisionLogCleanup
background_tasks.purpose: Remove old AI decision logs

background_tasks.name: FailedConditionCleanup
background_tasks.purpose: Remove noise indicator condition logs

background_tasks.name: FailedOrderCleanup
background_tasks.purpose: Remove old failed order records

background_tasks.name: RevokedTokenCleanup
background_tasks.purpose: Remove expired entries from revoked_tokens table

background_tasks.name: ReportCleanup
background_tasks.purpose: Remove generated reports older than 2 years

background_tasks.name: SessionCleanup
background_tasks.purpose: Expire stale active sessions and delete old inactive session records

background_tasks.name: RateLimitAttemptCleanup
background_tasks.purpose: Remove expired rate limit attempt records from database

background_tasks.name: AIOpinionLogCleanup
background_tasks.purpose: Delete trading.ai_opinion_log rows older than 90 days (matches the get_prior_ai_signals lookback clamp)

background_tasks.name: InMemoryCacheCleanup
background_tasks.purpose: Sweep all in-memory caches to prevent unbounded growth: price cache, chat/game rate-limit dicts, game rooms, monitor caches, auth rate limiters, public endpoint rate limiter, WebSocket stale connections

background_tasks.name: SpeculativeCalibrationMonitor
background_tasks.purpose: Analyze actual vs target hit-rate on closed speculative-bucket trades per account; email a calibration alert (via email_service.send_speculative_calibration_email) and broadcast a 'speculative_calibration_alert' WebSocket event when the recommended allocation diverges. Enforces a 30-day per-user cooldown via Account.speculative_calibration_last_alerted_at.
